acceptance_criteria: AC-01 [Session List]: GET /api/v1/daemon/agents returns an array where each item includes run_id, agent_id, task_id, phase, model, status, and elapsed_ms. Frontend /agents page renders one session card per item in the left panel.

AC-02 [Output Streaming]: SSE events with event_type 'agent_output_chunk' are rendered in the chat view for the session matching data.agent_id. Switching sessions clears the view and shows output for the new session within one SSE keepalive cycle (<=15s).

AC-03 [Deep-Link]: Navigating directly to /agents/:runId renders that session's output if runId is valid. An unknown runId shows a 'Session not found' message within the agents layout without a full-page error.

AC-04 [Output Rendering]: Given mixed AgentOutputChunk text content, the chat view renders: plain text as chat bubbles, JSON starting with {"kind": as collapsible decision cards, code fences as syntax-highlighted blocks, stderr text with an error indicator.

AC-05 [Reconnection]: When the SSE connection drops and reconnects, the Last-Event-ID header is sent with the last received sequence number. No duplicate output is shown in the chat view after reconnection.
AC-06 [Task Board Columns]: GET /api/v1/tasks data renders in five kanban columns: Backlog, Ready, In Progress, Blocked, Done. Column assignment matches task status field exactly.

AC-07 [Status Transition]: Changing a task's column calls POST /api/v1/tasks/:id/status with the new status. On success, the card appears in the new column without page reload. On failure, the card reverts and an error message is shown.

AC-08 [Workflow Pipeline]: GET /api/v1/workflows data renders each active workflow as a horizontal phase sequence (requirements -> research -> implementation -> code-review -> testing). The current phase node is visually highlighted.

AC-09 [Phase Decision Drill-Down]: Clicking a completed phase node fetches GET /api/v1/workflows/:id/decisions and shows the matching decision: verdict (advance/rework/fail), confidence (0.0-1.0), risk, and reason.
AC-10 [Dashboard Counts]: Dashboard shows total, in-progress, blocked, and done task counts from GET /api/v1/tasks/stats. Counts refresh without page reload on SSE stats_update events.

AC-11 [Daemon Control]: Dashboard shows daemon status from GET /api/v1/daemon/health. Start/Stop buttons call POST /daemon/start and POST /daemon/stop. Status indicator updates within one SSE keepalive cycle.
AC-12 [Agent Badge]: Navigation bar shows count of active agents from GET /daemon/agents. Count updates when an agent_status_changed SSE event is received.
AC-13 [Responsive Layout]: All six pages render without layout overflow on viewports >=768px wide. Touch targets for interactive elements are >=44x44px.

AC-14 [Route Integrity]: All routes (/agents, /agents/:runId, /tasks, /workflows, /workflows/:id, /planning, /planning/vision, /planning/requirements, /dashboard, /settings) render without a full-page error on direct navigation (hard refresh). Unknown routes show the Not Found page.
description:
Redesign the AO web application with agent interaction as the primary experience. The current web app is a CRUD management interface for tasks, requirements, and workflows. The new design makes **agent chat the centerpiece** — users should spend most of their time watching agents work, steering their decisions, and reviewing output, not clicking through admin forms.

## Design Philosophy

The web app should feel like a **mission control for AI agents**, not a project management tool. The primary loop is:
1. See what agents are doing right now (Agent Chat)
2. Review their decisions and output (Workflow Monitor)
3. Queue up more work (Task Board)
4. Plan what needs to happen (Planning Hub)

## Page Structure

### 1. Agent Chat (Primary — `/agents`)
The default landing page. Split into:
- **Session List** (left panel): All active agent runs, grouped by task. Shows agent model, phase, elapsed time, and status indicator (thinking, writing, idle, error).
- **Chat View** (main panel): Selected agent's live output stream in a conversational layout:
  - Agent thinking/reasoning shown in collapsible blocks with distinct styling
  - Code changes shown as inline diffs with syntax highlighting
  - JSON decisions (PhaseDecision, research_required, implementation_result) rendered as structured cards
  - File modifications shown with before/after previews
  - Error output highlighted with stack trace formatting
- **Context Sidebar** (right panel, collapsible): Task details, linked requirements, phase pipeline progress, decision history for current workflow.
- **Chat Input** (bottom, full vision only): Send instructions/guidance to the running agent. MVP: read-only, no input.
- **Session URL**: `/agents/:runId` deep-links to a specific session.

### 2. Task Board (`/tasks`)
Kanban-style board replacing the current list view:
- Columns: Backlog | Ready | In Progress | Blocked | Done
- Cards show: title, priority badge, assignee, linked workflow status indicator
- Drag-and-drop between columns triggers POST /api/v1/tasks/:id/status
- Click card → slide-out detail panel (client-side state, no navigation)
- Quick actions: assign, set priority, start workflow, add dependency
- Filter bar: by priority, type, assignee, tag, requirement
- "New Task" button opens inline creation form

### 3. Workflow Monitor (`/workflows`)
Real-time workflow execution dashboard:
- Active workflows shown as horizontal phase pipelines (requirements → research → implementation → code-review → testing)
- Each phase node shows: status (pending/running/passed/failed/rework), duration, agent model, decision verdict
- Click a phase → opens agent chat for that phase's run
- Decision gate visualization: confidence meter, risk indicator, evidence list, verdict (advance/rework/fail)
- Rework loops shown as cycle indicators on the pipeline
- Completed workflows shown in a timeline below

### 4. Planning Hub (`/planning`)
Consolidated planning interface:
- **Vision** tab: Vision document editor with AI refinement
- **Requirements** tab: Requirement list with status pipeline, priority matrix, acceptance criteria editor
- **Generate** tab: AI-assisted task generation from requirements — select requirements, generate task breakdown, review and approve
- Links between requirements → tasks → workflows visible as breadcrumb trails

### 5. Dashboard (`/dashboard`)
At-a-glance operational metrics:
- Active agents count with model breakdown
- Queue depth (ready tasks waiting for agents)
- Recent decisions (last 10) with verdict/confidence sparkline
- Throughput: tasks completed per hour/day (full vision — requires new metrics endpoint)
- Error rate and recent failures (full vision — requires new metrics endpoint)
- Daemon health status with uptime
- Quick actions: start/stop daemon, pause scheduling

### 6. Settings (`/settings`)
- Daemon configuration (max agents, interval, timeouts) (full vision)
- Agent runtime config editor (model selection, phase directives) (full vision)
- Storage backend selection (json-file, sqlite, postgres) (full vision)
- Notification preferences (full vision)
- MVP: read-only display of current config

## Agent Chat Interaction Model

### Session List
Fetched via `GET /api/v1/daemon/agents`. Polled every 10s and updated immediately
on `agent_status_changed` SSE events. Each session shows: run_id, task title (via
task_id lookup), phase, model, elapsed time, status.

### Output Streaming
The SSE stream at `/api/v1/events` broadcasts `AgentOutputChunk` events with
`agent_id`, `stream_type` (stdout/stderr/system), and `text`. The frontend filters
by `agent_id` for the selected session. Reconnection uses `Last-Event-ID` for
sequence-based resumption (no duplication).

### Output Rendering Pipeline
| Content | Rendered as |
|---|---|
| stdout plain text | Chat message bubble |
| stdout JSON with {"kind": prefix | Collapsible decision card |
| stdout code fences | Syntax-highlighted code block |
| stderr text | Error banner |
| event_type: thinking | Collapsible reasoning block |
| event_type: tool_call | Tool invocation card |
| event_type: artifact | File artifact preview |

### Bidirectional Messaging (Full Vision)
Requires a new WebSocket endpoint at `WS /api/v1/agents/:runId/chat` and changes
to the agent-runner IPC protocol to inject messages into running sessions. This
is deferred beyond MVP.

## SSE Data Contracts

### AgentOutputChunk event (event_type: 'agent_output_chunk')
```json
{
  "schema": "ao.daemon.v1.event",
  "seq": 1234,
  "timestamp": "2026-02-27T16:00:00Z",
  "event_type": "agent_output_chunk",
  "data": {
    "agent_id": "runner-abc123",
    "stream_type": "stdout",
    "text": "Analyzing the codebase..."
  }
}
```

### AgentStatusChanged event (event_type: 'agent_status_changed')
```json
{
  "event_type": "agent_status_changed",
  "data": {
    "agent_id": "runner-abc123",
    "status": "running",
    "elapsed_ms": 12500
  }
}
```

### GET /api/v1/daemon/agents response (per session)
```json
{
  "run_id": "wf-<wf_id>-implementation-claude-<uuid>",
  "agent_id": "runner-abc123",
  "task_id": "TASK-065",
  "phase": "implementation",
  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
  "tool": "claude",
  "status": "running",
  "elapsed_ms": 12500,
  "started_at": "2026-02-27T16:00:00Z"
}
```

## MVP vs Full Vision Phasing

### MVP (Phase 1) — Implementable with existing API
- Agent Chat: session list + one-way SSE output streaming + deep-link URL
- Task Board: kanban visual layout using existing tasks API
- Workflow Monitor: phase pipeline visualization using workflows + decisions API
- Planning Hub: existing largely implemented; UI refinements only
- Dashboard: task counts + daemon health (no throughput metrics)
- Navigation: agent count badge

### Full Vision (Phase 2) — Requires new API surface
- Bidirectional agent messaging via WebSocket
- Dashboard throughput and error rate metrics (new endpoints)
- Settings page with config read/write (new endpoints)
- Agent output history for completed sessions

## API Gap Analysis

Gaps identified against current `/api/v1` surface:
- `daemon/agents` response must include `phase` and `task_id` fields (verify/add)
- No `/metrics/throughput` or `/metrics/errors` endpoints (full vision)
- No `/settings` API (full vision)
- No WebSocket endpoint (full vision)
- `GET /api/v1/workflows` `current_phase_index` field needed for pipeline position

## Technical Constraints
- Must use existing Axum web server and embedded static assets pattern
- Must consume existing /api/v1 REST endpoints (extend as needed)
- SSE for real-time updates (existing /api/v1/events endpoint)
- WebSocket for bidirectional agent chat (new endpoint, full vision only)
- React + TypeScript frontend with Vite build
- No additional frontend framework dependencies beyond what exists

## Design Specification

See `crates/orchestrator-cli/docs/task-091-req-029-agent-chat-design.md` for:
- Full gap analysis table
- Agent Chat interaction model detail
- SSE data contract definitions
- MVP vs full vision feature table
- Testable acceptance criteria checklist
title: Agent-first web application redesign with chat-centric page structure
